title: quercus parvula var. shrevei
intro: Quercus parvula var. shrevei, also known as Shreve's oak, is a variety of the Quercus parvula tree, which is a small deciduous tree or shrub native to North America. The leaves of the Quercus parvula var. shrevei contain tannins and flavonoids, which have been shown to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus parvula var. shrevei include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of Quercus parvula var. shrevei can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
